subject: science
description: The atmosphere is primarily made up of air. Air has properties that can be observed and measured. The transfer of energy in the atmosphere causes air movement, which is felt as wind. Wind speed and direction can be measured.

subject: science
description: Water is present in the atmosphere. Water is present in the atmosphere as water vapor. When water vapor in the atmosphere cools, it forms clouds, fog, rain, ice, snow, sleet or hail. Note: The emphasis at this grade level is investigating condensation and evaporation, not memorizing the water cycle itself. Note: The emphasis is not in naming cloud types, but in relating the characteristics of the clouds with weather.

subject: science
description: Long- and short-term weather changes occur due to changes in energy. Changes in energy affect all aspects of weather, including temperature, precipitation, and wind.

subject: science
description: Forces change the motion of an object. Motion can increase, change direction or stop depending on the force applied. The change in motion of an object is related to the size of the force. Some forces act without touching, such as using a magnet to move an object or objects falling to the ground.

subject: science
description: Living things cause changes on Earth. Living things function and interact with their physical environments. Living things cause changes in the environments where they live; the changes can be very noticeable or slightly noticeable, fast or slow. Note: At this grade level, discussion is limited to changes that can be easily observed.

subject: science
description: All organisms alive today result from their ancestors, some of which may be extinct. Not all kinds of organisms that lived in the past are represented by living organisms today. Some kinds of organisms become extinct when their basic needs are no longer met or the environment changes.

subject: social_studies
description: Time can be shown graphically on calendars and timelines.

subject: social_studies
description: Change over time can be shown with artifacts, maps, and photographs.

subject: social_studies
description: Science and technology have changed daily life.

subject: social_studies
description: Biographies can show how peoples’ actions have shaped the world in which we live.

subject: social_studies
description: Maps and their symbols, including cardinal directions, can be interpreted to answer questions about location of places.

subject: social_studies
description: The work that people do is impacted by the distinctive human and physical characteristics in the place where they live.

subject: social_studies
description: Human activities alter the physical environment, both positively and negatively.

subject: social_studies
description: Cultures develop in unique ways, in part through the influence of the physical environment.

subject: social_studies
description: Interactions among cultures lead to sharing ways of life.

subject: social_studies
description: Respect for the rights of self and others includes making responsible choices and being accountable for personal actions.

subject: social_studies
description: Groups are accountable for choices they make and actions they take.

subject: social_studies
description: There are different rules and laws that govern behavior in different settings.

subject: social_studies
description: Information displayed on bar graphs can be used to compare quantities.

subject: social_studies
description: Resources can be used in various ways.

subject: social_studies
description: Most people around the world work in jobs in which they produce specific goods and services.

subject: social_studies
description: People use money to buy and sell goods and services.

subject: social_studies
description: People earn income by working.
